name: Indonesia
description: The world's largest archipelago — 17,000+ islands, 130 volcanoes, four religions, one independence movement.

questions.q: What is the de facto capital of Indonesia?

source.notes: Indonesia is moving its capital to Nusantara on Borneo, but Jakarta remains the largest city and de facto seat of government.

questions.q: Approximately how many islands make up the Indonesian archipelago?
Which Indonesian island is the most populous in the world?

source.notes: Around 150 million people live on Java — over half of Indonesia's total population.

questions.q: On what date did Indonesia proclaim independence?

source.notes: Two days after Japan's WWII surrender. Recognized by the Netherlands in 1949.

questions.q: Who was Indonesia's first president?
What was the name of the Dutch colonial entity that ruled Indonesia for centuries?
Which trading company dominated Indonesia for nearly 200 years before being dissolved in 1799?

source.notes: Verenigde Oostindische Compagnie. Bankrupt by 1799; assets taken over by the Dutch government.

questions.q: Which Indonesian leader's authoritarian 'New Order' regime ran from 1967 to 1998?

source.notes: Forced from office during the 1997 Asian Financial Crisis.

questions.q: Which territory voted for independence from Indonesia in 1999?

source.notes: Indonesia had occupied East Timor since 1975. Independence formalized in 2002.

questions.q: Which large Pacific island is shared between Indonesia and Papua New Guinea?

source.notes: World's second-largest island. Indonesian half = Western New Guinea / Papua.

questions.q: Which Indonesian island is named after the giant lizard found nowhere else?

source.notes: The Komodo dragon is the world's largest living lizard.

questions.q: What is Rafflesia arnoldii, native to Indonesia and famously found there?

source.notes: Single blooms can reach 1 metre across. Smells like rotting flesh — hence 'corpse flower'.

questions.q: Approximately how many active volcanoes does Indonesia have?

source.notes: More active volcanoes than any other country.

questions.q: What Indonesian lake, the world's largest volcanic lake, was formed by a supervolcanic eruption ~74,000 years ago?

source.notes: Located on Sumatra. The eruption may have caused a 1,000-year global volcanic winter.

questions.q: Which 9th-century Buddhist temple in Central Java is one of the largest in the world?
What agricultural commodity is Indonesia the world's largest producer of?

source.notes: Indonesia produces over half the world's palm oil.

questions.q: What is the world's largest archipelagic state by both island count and population?
Which Indonesian island, with Lake Toba and orangutans, is the country's westernmost large island?
What is the name of Indonesia's planned new capital, currently under construction on Borneo?

source.notes: Move announced in 2019 to relieve sinking, traffic-choked Jakarta.

questions.q: Which fossil hominid, discovered in 1891, was called 'Java Man'?

source.notes: Discovered by Dutch anatomist Eugène Dubois on the island of Java.
